children.name: ParseCommand
children.purpose: Validate and parse the command string to determine the operation type.
children.behavior: Check if command is one of 'place', 'cancel', 'track'. If invalid, return error. Otherwise, pass command type forward.

constraints.description: Must return error string for invalid commands

acceptance_criteria.description: Given 'place', returns 'place'
Given 'cancel', returns 'cancel'
Given 'track', returns 'track'
Given invalid command, returns error string

children.name: ValidateOrderData
children.purpose: Validate the order_data structure and required fields based on command type.
children.behavior: Check that order_data contains required fields (e.g., items for place, order_id for cancel/track). Return validation result.
children.signature: def ValidateOrderData(command: str, order_data: dict) -> dict

constraints.description: Must validate required fields based on command type

acceptance_criteria.description: Given 'place' and valid order_data, returns {'valid': True}
Given 'place' and missing items, returns {'valid': False, 'error': '...'}
Given 'cancel' and missing order_id, returns {'valid': False, 'error': '...'}

children.name: PlaceOrder
children.purpose: Execute the 'place' command: validate items, charge payment, reserve inventory, create order, send notification.
children.behavior: Sequentially: check item existence and stock, charge payment, reserve inventory, create order record, send notification. On failure, rollback previous steps.

data_operations.description: Create new order record with status 'confirmed'

constraints.description: Must rollback all changes on failure

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid order_data, returns success with order_id
Given insufficient stock, returns failure and no changes made

children.name: CancelOrder
children.purpose: Execute the 'cancel' command: verify order exists and not shipped, refund payment, restore inventory, update order status.
children.behavior: Check order existence and status, refund payment, restore reserved inventory, update order status to cancelled. Rollback on failure.

global_vars.description: Read order status, update to cancelled
Read payment record, update status to refunded

data_operations.description: Verify order exists and status is not shipped

constraints.description: Must rollback all changes on failure
Cannot cancel order that has been shipped

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid order_id for unshipped order, returns success with status 'cancelled'
Given shipped order, returns failure

children.name: TrackOrder
children.purpose: Execute the 'track' command: return current order status and estimated delivery time.
children.behavior: Look up order by order_id in orders store, retrieve status and estimated delivery time, return result.

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid order_id, returns success with status and delivery time
Given invalid order_id, returns failure with error message

children.name: CheckItemAvailability
children.purpose: Validate that all items in the order exist and have sufficient stock (including reserved count).
children.behavior: For each item in order_data['items'], check inventory store for existence and stock_count - reserved_count >= quantity. Return boolean and error message.

global_vars.description: Read stock_count and reserved_count for each item

data_operations.description: Check item existence and available stock

acceptance_criteria.description: Given items with sufficient stock, returns {'available': True}
Given items with insufficient stock, returns {'available': False, 'error': '...'}

children.name: ChargePayment
children.purpose: Charge the payment for the order using the specified payment method.
children.behavior: Create a payment record with amount calculated from items, charge via payment gateway (simulated), update payment status. Return payment_id or failure.
children.signature: def ChargePayment(items: list, payment_method: str) -> dict

constraints.description: Must calculate total amount from items

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid items and payment method, returns success with payment_id
Given invalid payment method, returns failure

children.name: ReserveInventory
children.purpose: Reserve inventory for the order items by incrementing reserved_count.
children.behavior: For each item, update inventory store: reserved_count += quantity. Return success or failure with rollback capability.

constraints.description: Must be atomic across all items

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid items, returns success and reserved_count incremented
Given invalid items, returns failure and no changes

children.name: CreateOrderRecord
children.purpose: Create the order record in the orders data store with status 'confirmed'.
children.behavior: Generate order_id if not provided, store order details including items, payment_id, shipping_address, status, created_at.
children.signature: def CreateOrderRecord(order_data: dict, payment_id: str) -> dict

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid order_data and payment_id, returns success with order_id
Order record contains all required fields

children.name: SendNotification
children.purpose: Send a confirmation notification to the customer (simulated).
children.behavior: Compose and send notification message based on order details. Log notification. Return success.
children.signature: def SendNotification(order_id: str, order_data: dict) -> dict

constraints.description: Must not modify any data store

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid order_id and order_data, returns success
Notification is logged (simulated)

governance_notes: Parent global_vars conservation verified: orders (read_write) covered by PlaceOrder (write), CancelOrder (read_write), TrackOrder (read), CreateOrderRecord (write). inventory (read_write) covered by PlaceOrder (read_write), CancelOrder (write), CheckItemAvailability (read), ReserveInventory (write). payments (read_write) covered by PlaceOrder (write), CancelOrder (read_write), ChargePayment (write). All parent operations are fully covered by child global_vars.
